title: eriogonum kennedyi subsp. austromontanum
intro: Eriogonum kennedyi subsp. austromontanum, also known as Kennedy's buckwheat, is a rare perennial herb native to the southwestern United States. This plant is a type of buckwheat relative and is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum kennedyi subsp. austromontanum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. It can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
